text.title: What Each Transport Implies for Auth, Scaling, and Lifecycle
text.summary: stdio = one process per session, no network auth; streamable-http = LB + OAuth 2.1 + resumable streams; mismatched shape blocks production.

rule.statement: If you choose stdio, document that the server is single-user, in-process-tree, and auth is delegated to the host OS user. Do NOT add OAuth, do NOT bind to a port, do NOT log to a shared file — assume the parent process owns lifecycle and stdout/stderr.
rule.rationale: stdio servers inherit the client's identity. Adding network auth on top of stdio is wasted work; binding to a port turns a local subprocess into a quasi-network surface that bypasses the client's sandboxing.

section.title: streamable-http — remote scalable shape

rule.statement: If you choose Streamable HTTP, you MUST implement: (1) OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for client auth, (2) idempotent request handling so a resumed SSE stream does not double-execute side effects, (3) a single `/mcp` endpoint that accepts POST and upgrades to SSE per request, (4) horizontal-scale-safe session state (Redis or DB, not in-process).
rule.rationale: The Streamable HTTP transport is designed to support multi-tenant hosted deployments. Skipping OAuth opens the server to anyone; in-process session state makes the deployment a single point of failure.

section.reference: 2026 MCP roadmap — Streamable HTTP as the default remote transport.

antipattern.description: Server uses stdio but is wrapped by a TCP-to-stdio bridge to "make it remote".
antipattern.reason: The bridge becomes a single-point bottleneck with no auth, no resumability, and no per-tenant isolation. Use Streamable HTTP natively instead.

antipattern.description: Streamable HTTP server keeps session state in a Python dict (in-process).
antipattern.reason: Two replicas behind an LB cannot see each other's sessions; the next request hits the wrong replica and the agent loses context mid-loop.

example.code: # Production-shape Streamable HTTP MCP service service: transport: streamable-http endpoint: /mcp auth: oauth2.1-pkce session_store: redis://valkey:6379/0 replicas: 3 health: /healthz
example.note: Stateless replicas + shared session store + OAuth — the minimum production shape.
